---
title: "Chapter 1 - WT vs pifq"
subtitle: "Single-cell RNA-seq processing for Sc_DB_test"
author: "SingleCell pipeline"
date: "2026-07-15"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Overview

This chapter documents the WT vs pifq single-cell workflow prepared in:

```text
/home/mvergara/projects2/Sc_DB_test
```

All outputs are written to:

```text
/home/mvergara/projects2/Sc_DB_test/resultados_wt
```

The executable workflow is:

```text
SingleCell/workflow/capitulo1_wt_pifq.R
```

To rerun the analysis:

```bash
cd /home/mvergara/projects2/Sc_DB_test
bash run_cap1_wt_pifq.sh
```

# Cell Ranger input

The downstream analysis starts from existing Cell Ranger filtered matrices.

| sample | condition | Cell Ranger ID | matrix |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| WT | WT | `ScWT` | `ScWT/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix` |
| pifq | pifq | `Scpifq` | `Scpifq/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix` |

Cell Ranger processing is documented in:

- `cell_CRA010863_runs.sh`
- `cell_CRA010863_ensure_ScWT.sh`

The command pattern used by those scripts was:

```bash
cellranger count \
  --localcores=80 \
  --id=<ScWT|Scpifq> \
  --fastqs=/home/mvergara/projects2/Sc_DB_test/CRA010863 \
  --sample=<ScWT|Scpifq> \
  --transcriptome=/home/mvergara/projects2/eleo/ftp1.cruk.cam.ac.uk/Seba/Ara \
  --no-bam
```

| sample | estimated cells | mean reads/cell | median genes/cell | reads mapped to transcriptome | total genes detected | median UMI/cell |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| WT | 4,067 | 91,809 | 1,119 | 88.4% | 24,769 | 2,766 |
| pifq | 7,006 | 57,449 | 1,192 | 86.3% | 25,380 | 2,824 |

# Minimum computing requirements

The downstream Seurat/R workflow was executed in Docker using `matigara/scrnaseq:latest`.

| component | minimum practical requirement |
| --- | --- |
| CPU | 8 cores |
| RAM | 64 GB |
| disk | 50 GB free for matrices, objects, PDFs and h5ad export |
| runtime | Docker with access to the project folder |

For Cell Ranger processing from raw FASTQ files, use a server or external cluster. The local project scripts use `--localcores=80`, which is more appropriate for a high-memory server than a personal laptop.

# Workflow parameters

| step | parameter |
| --- | --- |
| QC filter | `min_features = 200`, `max_mt = 5` |
| mitochondrial pattern | `^ATMG` |
| chloroplast pattern | `^ATCG` |
| PCA/UMAP dimensions | `1:30` |
| batch correction | Harmony by `orig.ident` |
| resolution sweep | `0.15, 0.35, 0.45, 0.55, 1.0` |
| final resolution | `0.35` |
| clustering | Leiden, `algorithm = 4`, `k.param = 20` |
| annotation | `biblio_marks_custom.txt` |
| expression example | `AT5G26000` |

# Quality control

```{r, out.width="80%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/qc_prefilter.png")
```

```{r, out.width="80%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/qc_postfilter.png")
```

# Harmony integration

```{r, out.width="72%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/umap_preharmony.png")
```

```{r, out.width="72%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/umap_postharmony.png")
```

# Resolution optimization

```{r, out.width="55%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/elbow_plot.png")
```

```{r, out.width="82%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/clustree2.png")
```

# Final clustering

```{r, out.width="72%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/umap_seuratclusters.png")
```

# Bibliography marker annotation

The marker file used for annotation is:

```text
biblio_marks_custom.txt
```

```{r, out.width="72%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/umap_annotation_biblio.png")
```

```{r, out.width="82%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/dotplot_marker_table_annotation_biblio.png")
```

```{r, out.width="82%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/clustree_annotated.png")
```

Clusters with bibliography marker support were assigned to:

- `Epidermis Hypocotyl.1`
- `Epidermis Cotyledon`
- `Mesophyll`
- `Epidermis Hypocotyl.2`
- `PHLOEM`
- `ENDO1`
- `C2`
- `guard cell`

The labels `2`, `4`, `5`, and `7` remained numeric because those clusters did not match the bibliography marker table in this run.

# Gene expression example

The simple expression block uses one gene: `AT5G26000`.

```{r, out.width="72%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/feature_gene_all.png")
```

```{r, out.width="72%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/vln_gene_all.png")
```

# Grouping and optional curation

The current run keeps `celltype_grouped` as a pass-through of `celltype`. Optional manual curation can start from:

```text
resultados_wt/05_curacion/section_11_subcluster_inspection_template.R
```

```{r, out.width="72%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("../../resultados_wt/report_assets/umap_grouped.png")
```

# Final objects

| file | description |
| --- | --- |
| `resultados_wt/objects/pbmc_harmony_curated.rds` | final Seurat object |
| `resultados_wt/objects/pbmc_harmony_curated.h5ad` | exported object for Python/Scanpy |
| `resultados_wt/03_annotation/FindAllMarkers.tsv` | marker table |
| `resultados_wt/sessionInfo.txt` | complete R session information |

Final dimensions:

| metric | value |
| --- | ---: |
| genes | 23,657 |
| cells | 8,005 |
| WT cells | 2,545 |
| pifq cells | 5,460 |

# Software versions

| software/package | version |
| --- | --- |
| R | 4.5.3 |
| Seurat | 5.5.0 |
| SeuratObject | 5.4.0 |
| DoubletFinder | 2.0.6 |
| harmony | 2.0.3 |
| clustree | 0.5.1 |
| ggplot2 | 4.0.3 |
| dplyr | 1.2.1 |
| Matrix | 1.7-5 |
| SingleCellExperiment | 1.32.0 |
| zellkonverter | 1.20.1 |
| anndataR | 1.0.2 |

Full package versions are recorded in `resultados_wt/sessionInfo.txt`.
